section.title: Introduction and Overview
section.p: Acorn Access provides peer-to-peer networking using the TCP/IP protocol, allowing sharing of resources such as discs and printers.
It has been provided as part of the operating system from RISC OS 3.6 onwards.

subsection.title: Access components
subsection.p: There are three main modules that make up Access: Freeway, ShareFS and RemotePrinterSupport.

category.title: Freeway
category.p: Freeway provides the protocols used by Access so it knows when shared resources are available and can display windows showing them. The interfaces used by Freeway are for internal use only; you must not use them in your own code.

category.title: ShareFS
category.p: ShareFS is a filing system that is used to share resources, both granting other hosts access to your machine, and vice versa.

ShareFS provides *commands that you can use to share your own filing systems with other Access users. It also provides SWIs that you can use to add a Share option to a Filer's icon bar menu; see the section titled 'Writing Filers so they integrate with Access'Should be a reference.

category.title: RemotePrinterSupport
category.p: RemotePrinterSupport provides the support needed to share printers over an Access network.
The interfaces used by RemotePrinterSupport are for internal use only; you must not use them in your own code.

subsection.title: Writing filing systems so they integrate with Access
subsection.p: The Access * Commands call standard entry points to filing systems when making them shared. You do not need to take any special steps to make a filing system work with Access; any filing system will work, provided it conforms to the specifications in the chapters 'Writing a filing system'; and (where applicable) 'Writing a FileCore module'.

subsection.title: Writing Filers so they integrate with Access
subsection.p: For a Filer to integrate with Access, it needs to provide a Share menu option, and take appropriate action when the option is chosen. This is done using a SWI interface to ShareFS.

section.title: Technical Details
section.p: How it does stuff, and how you talk to it.

use.p: This service is issued by systems sharing objects to other machines, and should not be claimed. The private data is dependent upon the FS name. For ShareFS these are:
In R5, the ShareFS flags:

bitfield-table.bit: CD ROM (ReadOnly, with a different icon)

use.p: Pin (for authentication), or 0 if not authenticating.
This service is issued on a callback after ShareFS has initialised.

service-definition.name: ShareFSTerminating
service-definition.description: ShareFS is about to shut down

use.p: This service is issued before ShareFS is shut down. It is not issued prior to ShareFS 3.73.

entry.register-use: Flags which are horrid and should be reviewed before inclusion
Pointer to name to share as

use.p: This SWI shares a directory with the specified attributes. Beware the flags in R0 are silly, and the API sketchily documented at present.
This SWI stops sharing a given disc.

entry.register-use:
0 to match all shares, or flags as follows :
Protected (public access attributes are obeyed) Read only Hidden (doesn't appear in display) Sub directory (must be set if b31 set) CD ROM (ReadOnly, with a different icon) Passworded
0 initially, or opaque value returned in R4

exit.register-use: Pointer to read only shared disc name
Pointer to read only path name

Flags :
Protected (public access attributes are obeyed) Read only Hidden (doesn't appear in display) Sub directory (must be set if b31 set) CD ROM (ReadOnly, with a different icon) Passworded

use.p: This SWI is used to list the shares being made available from this machine.

swi-definition.name: ShareFS_IndentifyShare
swi-definition.description: Identify whether a directory or share is known to ShareFS

entry.register-use:
flags :
R1 points to a share name,
otherwise R1 points to a path name
pointer to share or path name

exit.register-use:
flags for share :
Protected (public access attributes are obeyed) Read only Hidden (doesn't appear in display) Sub directory (must be set if b31 set) CD ROM (ReadOnly, with a different icon) Passworded
length written to buffer, or -ve size if not enough space.

use.p: This SWI is used to convert from a directory or share name, to a share or directory name respectively. If no corresponding share is known, an error is returned.

message-table.message:
Flags:
Protected (public access attributes are obeyed) Read Only Hidden (doesn't appear in display) Password (blank password initially) CD ROM (ReadOnly, with a different icon) Open window at position given Open window as a submenu (not implemented)

use.p: This message is sent by an application which wishes to present the user with a set of options for sharing a directory. A window will be opened either around the pointer, or at the position requested.

command-definition.name: Desktop_ShareFSFiler
command-definition.description: Command to start up ShareFS Filer

use.p: *Desktop_... commands are used by the Desktop to start up ROM-resident Desktop utilities that appear automatically on the iconbar. However, they are for internal use only, and you should not use them; use instead.

command-definition.name: Dismount
command-definition.description: Ensures that it is safe to finish using a remote shared disc
command-definition.parameter: The name of the remote shared disc.

use.p: *Dismount ensures that it is safe to finish using a remote shared disc by closing all its file, unsetting all its directories and libraries, and discarding its local caches. *Dismount is useful before finishing sharing a particular disc. However, the *Shutdown command is usually to be preferred, especially when switching off the computer.

command-definition.name: Free
command-definition.description: Displays the total free space remaining on a remote shared disc
command-definition.parameter: The name of the remote shared disc

use.p: *Free displays the total free space remaining on a remote shared disc.

command-definition.name: FwShow
command-definition.description: Displays all currently known Freeway objects

use.p: *FwShow displays all currently known Freeway objects. Local objects are prefixed with a '*'. Merge this entry to the freeway document ?

command-definition.name: Share
command-definition.description: Makes a local directory available as a shared disc
command-definition.parameter: A valid pathname specifying a directory.
The name to use for the shared disc.
Causes the directory to be shared protected, rather than the default of unprotected
Indicated that the shared directory is on a CD-ROM.
Prevents an icon appearing for the shared disc.

use.p: *Share makes a local directory available as a shared disc. If no name is given for the shared disc, then the name of the directory is used, or - for the root directory - the name of the disc itself.

If the directory is shared unprotected, then remote users have read and write access to all objects beneath it. If the directory is shared protected, then remote users' access to an object beneath it is determined by that object's public access attributes.

command-definition.name: ShareFS
command-definition.description: Selects the Shared Filing System as the current filing system

use.p: ShareFS selects the Shared Filing System as the filing system for subsequent operations. Remember that it is not neccessary to switch filing systems if you use the full pathnames of objects. For example, you can refer to ADFS objects (on a local disc), when ShareFS is the current filing system.

command-definition.name: ShareFSIcon
command-definition.description: Adds an icon to the icon bar for a remote shared disc
command-definition.parameter: The name of the remote shared disc

use.p: *ShareFSIcon adds an icon to the icon bar for a remote shared disc.

command-definition.name: ShareFSWindow
command-definition.description: Changes or reports the size of the ShareFS transmission window
command-definition.parameter: The size of the ShareFS transmission window.

use.p: *ShareFSWindow changes the size of the ShareFS transmission window, or - with no parameter - reports its current size. You should not change the size unless you know what you are doing.

command-definition.name: Shares
command-definition.description: Shares lists the local directories currently made available as shared discs

use.p: *Shares lists the local directories currently made available as shared discs, showing the full *Share command with which it was shared.

command-definition.name: UnShare
command-definition.description: UnShare makes a local directory no longer available as a shared disc
command-definition.parameter: The name of the remote shared disc.

use.p: *UnShare makes a local directory no longer available as a shared disc.

revision.author: ROL
revision.title: Little tidying; not ready for distribution
revision.change: Updated some entry and exit conditions to reflect the processor and interrupt state.

revision.author: ROL
revision.title: Removed redundant section
revision.change: An error-definition section had been left behind; now removed.

revision.author: ROL
revision.title: Finished SWIs
revision.change: EnumerateShares and IdentifyShare had been incomplete, this change finishes their documentation.
